topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The summons landed on my desk with a heavy thud, the official seal looking like a brand. My hands, usually steady enough to ace any exam, began to tremble as I unfolded the document. Accusation of plagiarism. My stomach plummeted. I reread the highlighted passages, the accusations laid out in cold, damning prose. A slow, creeping disbelief began to coil in my chest. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and stared at the text as if it were written in an alien language. "This can't be right," I whispered, the sound thin and reedy in the quiet room. I pushed the papers away, needing to physically move away from the sheer absurdity of the situation.

[story 2]

When Professor Albright finally laid the evidence on my desk, the air in the office seemed to thicken, pressing down on my lungs. I watched the meticulously cited paragraphs, the direct quotations that now pointed squarely at my name. My jaw went slack. I blinked several times, trying to process the sheer volume of the claim. A strange, hollow feeling settled over me, a sudden absence of breath. I picked up the pen, intending to write a defense, but my fingers felt clumsy, disconnected from my intent. "Excuse me?" I managed, the words coming out as a dry croak. I looked up at the professor, my eyes wide, searching for some misplaced clue that would explain this impossible situation.

[story 3]

I sat in the sterile waiting room, the fluorescent lights humming an irritating, relentless tune. The file they handed me felt impossibly heavy, the paper cool beneath my fingertips. Every line was a hammer blow against my understanding of the last few months. I traced a sentence with my gaze, my pupils dilating slightly as I tried to reconcile the work I had poured into that paper with the accusations presented. A sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped me. I gripped the edge of the cheap chair until my knuckles turned white. "I... I don't understand," I stammered, my voice wavering uncontrollably. The world outside the window seemed to blur, focusing entirely on the impossible reality unfolding before me.
